name: CRM System
professionalExpectations: A CRM must make account, contact, opportunity, task, and activity state clear without spreadsheet-style hunting.
Record ownership, status, timestamps, and next actions must be visible on core screens.
Workflows must support repeat use by operators who need speed, filtering, and auditability.
Integrations must be optional and gated; the first version must work with local or repository-backed records.
minimumQualityBar: Core entities and status transitions are explicit and validated.
No live imports, exports, email sync, SMS, payment, or production data connection exists without approval.
Read and write paths are separated so future approval gates can control risky operations.
Role assumptions and data classification are documented before customer data is handled.
commonModules: Account and contact records with ownership, status, notes, and activity history.
Pipeline or opportunity tracking with stages, next actions, and close probability only when useful.
Task queue, reminders, filters, saved views, and audit events for operator activity.
Import/export planning, connector gates, and data retention rules for future live use.
uxExpectations: Dense, scannable tables with clear filters, saved views, and quick detail panels.
Status and next action controls must be obvious without decorative marketing layout.
Forms should be compact, validated, and resilient to partial entry.
Audit and ownership fields should be visible to operators without crowding the main workflow.

components: Repository records or local JSON for first model validation
Static or lightweight app shell for read-only review
Existing Postgres only after approved runtime phase
GitHub for source and PR governance
Netlify only after approved staging or production deployment
recommendedStack.notes: Start offline and schema-first. Do not connect email, SMS, billing, third-party CRM tools, or production databases until owner-approved.

phases.name: Data Model And Workflow
goals: Define core entities, statuses, ownership, and audit needs.
Map read, write, import, and export surfaces with approval gates.

phases.name: Operator Interface
goals: Build list, detail, filters, task queue, and status review views.
Keep the first interface local or repository-backed until live storage is approved.

phases.name: Controlled Runtime
goals: Add approved storage, connector, and audit behavior only after validation and rollback planning.
Introduce production data handling only after data classification and backup rules pass.

knownPitfalls: Overbuilding custom automation before the core operator workflow is stable.
Treating CRM data as low-risk when it may become customer or production data.
Adding imports without deduplication, validation, backup, or rollback rules.
Hiding ownership and audit fields from the people who must maintain the system.
approvalGates: Owner approval is required before connecting live email, SMS, CRM, payment, database, or automation services.
Owner approval is required before importing, exporting, or transforming production or customer data.
Owner approval is required before deployment, domain or DNS change, paid tool use, or credential handling.
Owner approval and rollback planning are required before destructive bulk edits or migrations.
qualityChecklist: Core entities, statuses, required fields, and transitions are validated.
Operator workflows can be completed without live services.
Tables, filters, and detail views are responsive and accessible.
Audit, ownership, data classification, backup, and rollback expectations are documented.
whatToBuildFirst: Read-only list/detail interface for representative record shapes without production data.
Audit and approval-gate map for future live actions.
whatNotToOverbuildEarly: Custom sales forecasting, AI scoring, or complex automation before core data quality is proven.
Live imports from email, spreadsheets, or existing CRMs before approval and backup planning.
Permission matrix beyond the owner and documented future roles.
Paid CRM connectors or enrichment services before budget approval.
stopConditions: Stop before handling credentials, production data, customer data, live imports, live exports, or paid enrichment.
Stop before connecting email, SMS, CRM, database, n8n, Netlify, domain, DNS, or deployment services.
Stop before touching the existing Lead Gen production system or AI Receptionist repo without separate approval.
notes: Archetype knowledge record only. It does not authorize live CRM operation, data import, outreach, or production changes.
